name: Chris Van Hollen
subject: This is a fight for our future and the future of our kids
disclaimer_text: Paid for by the DSCC, dscc
body:
Over the past year, Democrats like you have renewed my faith in the power of our democracy
---
[](http://action.dscc. "https://secure.actblue.com/donate/support-democrats?refcode=DPE-20180301-VanHollen-ProsHV&recurring=auto&amounts=5,10,25,50,100,150,250&amount=5&firstname=&lastname=&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&addr1= &city=&state=&zip=&phone=&employer=&occupation=")
Team --

Over the past year, Democrats like you have renewed my faith in the power of our democracy to overcome setbacks and move our country forward -- and I just wanted to say thank you.

Since Trump took office, Democrats have flipped 39 Republican-held state legislative seats from red to blue, and countless more at the local municipal level. And let's not forget our historic Senate victory in deep-red Alabama.

This record shows that Democrats can win anywhere when the full force of grassroots donations, grassroots organizing, and grassroots activism is behind them.

Each of you have been a part of that, from signing petitions, to making calls, to donating critical resources to power Democrats to victory - and I want to thank you for your continuing support and engagement in this critical fight.

**We couldn 't do this without you. Being right on the issues is not enough -- we need to fight back in the trenches. We need to push back against the negative onslaught funded by GOP SuperPacs and billionaires like the Koch Brothers against my 26 Senate colleagues who are up for re-election this year.**

Senate Democrats are fighting hard to defend our values and stand up for everyday Americans against the extremist agenda we have seen from Donald Trump and the Republican controlled Congress. Their focus on passing legislation that benefits wealthy donors and big corporations, not lifting up working people has been sickening. Among other things, they must be held accountable for their willingness to back the NRA and the gun industry over the calls of the overwhelming majority of Americans for real action to pass common sense gun laws.

It's never been more important that we stand together to hold Republicans accountable. If we keep up the same level of energy and commitment, I believe we'll be in a strong position to do just that in November.

So thank you, and please stay engaged. It's vital that Democrats like you remain fired up and ready to finish this fight strong and take our country back this November.

Thank you for all you do,

Senator Chris Van Hollen
DSCC Chair

**Democratic Senatorial Campaign Committee

subject: Read this from a retired St. Louis police officer
disclaimer_text: Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
body:
Team,

Please see the below piece from Retired St. Louis Police Sgt. Tom Lake. Please share with your friends on Facebook and Twitter.

https://americanmilitarynews.com/2018/03/op-ed-how-the-st-louis-circuit-attorneys-anti-police-agenda-led-to-the-indictment-of-a-pro-cop-republican-governor/?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=4bfc862de7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-4bfc862de7-99411629

By: Ret. Sgt. Tom Lake
March 1, 2018

Some have been shocked at the indictment of Republican Governor Eric Greitens by St. Louis Circuit Attorney Kim Gardner. But there’s little reason to be: Kim Gardner is the most anti-law enforcement Circuit Attorney to ever hold that job.

Elected in a city that still seethes in the aftermath of the 2015 Ferguson protests, Kim Gardner’s anti-police and anti-law enforcement position actually won her votes with a portion of St. Louis’s population. So why she did decide to go after Missouri Governor Eric Greitens? That’s obvious: there are few Governors in the country as intensely pro-law enforcement as Greitens.

I speak from personal experience. In 2016, I was ambushed and shot in the face in the line of duty. From that day on, the Governor stood with me—visiting, texting, checking in on me and my family. He’s gone out of his way to make sure I had everything I needed, and to this day, it chokes me up thinking about his support. He did so much—and so consistently—that I’d often wonder how he stood by my side and did everything else required of his job.

His campaign was shaped by Ferguson and the riots. During his campaign, Greitens was deeply critical of the state response (https://www.themarshallproject.org/2016/10/16/ferguson-still-haunts-missouri-and-not-the-way-you-might-think?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=4bfc862de7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-4bfc862de7-99411629) to the Ferguson protests, arguing convincingly that cops had their hands tied during the protests by liberal politicians too squeamish to do the tough and right things to restore peace.

Since taking office, that’s exactly what he’s done. The Governor has been visible and vocal in his support of police officers and first responders—attending funerals (https://www.kshb.com/news/local-news/hundreds-gather-for-fallen-clinton-officers-funeral?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=4bfc862de7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-4bfc862de7-99411629) , rushing to bedsides after police shootings (http://www.stltoday.com/news/local/crime-and-courts/investigation-to-dissect-how-handcuffed-suspect-shot-arnold-officer/article_84eb3b0e-12ec-51a7-9f35-1d748fd9c616.html?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=4bfc862de7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-4bfc862de7-99411629) , passing laws that strengthen the penalties for assaults on law enforcement (http://www.kansascity.com/news/politics-government/article160007389.html?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=4bfc862de7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-4bfc862de7-99411629) , and through a range of messages and tweets and posts, making sure that the men and women who keep our streets safe know that they have a Governor who stands with them. My friends in law enforcement—to a person—have said some version of the following, “God, it’s good to finally have a Governor who has our backs.”

His pro-law enforcement credentials were on full display during last year’s ruling in the police shooting case of Officer Jason Stockley. Tensions ran high in St. Louis; many were calling for another round of Ferguson-style protests, promising to burn the city to the ground if Officer Stockley wasn’t convicted. National groups—including antifa—were mobilized and ready.

And the Governor had none of it. He deployed the National Guard early (http://www.stltoday.com/news/local/govt-and-politics/national-guard-troops-were-put-on-standby-before-stockley-verdict/article_8b978ee9-80d8-5beb-9401-3933653f9b3e.html?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=4bfc862de7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-4bfc862de7-99411629) , met with members of the clergy in the days running up the decision (http://www.stltoday.com/news/local/metro/video-greitens-meets-with-st-louis-clergy-ahead-of-stockley/html_c783578e-3b22-53a2-8b72-e5285d135ca4.html?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=4bfc862de7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-4bfc862de7-99411629) , set up a command center well before the verdict, and was himself seen traveling the streets and talking to law enforcement. A quote that traveled far and wide from that protest came from the Governor himself: “In Missouri, if you loot and riot, the only safe space you’re going to have is in a jail cell.”

How did Stockley turn out? A few broken windows; a few vandals hauled off to jail. No sustained and violent protests. Peace restored, in a matter of days.

Importantly, the Governor didn’t unnecessarily politicize that situation. He promised people peace in the streets, but he also assured people that their rights to protest would be protected. For those in law enforcement, the Stockley case wasn’t easy. The only reassuring thing about the whole ordeal was the Governor’s calm demeanor.

To Kim Gardner and her BLM supporters, Greitens represented a real threat: a Navy SEAL Governor calling for peace, but someone who also took action and who made no bones about enforcing the law and standing behind those who do.

Greitens needed to be stopped.

So Gardner did what politically-motivated prosecutors with an agenda do: she went sniffing for a crime the Governor had committed. Moments after news of an affair hit the headlines, Gardner had launched her investigation and sent a signal to her supporters: this is how we get him. From a more capable and thoughtful prosecutor, such an action might be taken seriously. But during her tenure as Circuit Attorney, Gardner has given the public ample reason to question her skills, credibility, and seriousness.

...

All of which casts serious doubt on the methods and the motives behind Gardner’s investigation of Gov. Eric Greitens. Already, serious doubts have been raised about whether Gardner has followed the law while enforcing the law. Her office reportedly took this case to the FBI, the US Attorney, and the local police. Each one declined to investigate, which should tell us all something about the case itself.

Because none of those bodies would look into it, she was forced to mysteriously hire outside, out-of-state private investigators (http://www.971talk.com/blogs/annie-frey-show/weekender/kim-gardner-uses-hired-guns-instead-police-greitens-investigation?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=4bfc862de7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-4bfc862de7-99411629) to speak to elected officials and others. Whether those people were registered to do that work and sworn in to do it remains a question that she refuses to answer. It is, as one public safety official put it, “totally nuts, and unlike anything I’ve ever seen in all my years of law enforcement.”

Nuts — perhaps. Except when you consider that Gardner isn’t looking to conduct a fair and impartial investigation into whether a law was violated. What she is doing is carrying water for Black Lives Matter, Antifa, George Soros, and the many other groups on whose support she depends. She sees a law-and-order Governor—and she attacks as only an anti-law enforcement prosecutor would.

There is, in fact, nothing shocking about Gardner’s indictment of Greitens. It is entirely in keeping with her politics and the politics of her supporters. The truly shocking thing will be if this kangaroo court is allowed to proceed. If so, law enforcement personnel around the country should be worried about what fringe elements can get away with. Law enforcement has had few better friends in high office than Governor Eric Greitens, and to those who share that view, and stand behind law enforcement themselves, it’s time to stand up to the forces trying to take a law-and-order Governor down.

Tom Lake is a retired St. Louis Police Department Sergeant.

----

See the full piece here: https://americanmilitarynews.com/2018/03/op-ed-how-the-st-louis-circuit-attorneys-anti-police-agenda-led-to-the-indictment-of-a-pro-cop-republican-governor/?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=4bfc862de7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-4bfc862de7-99411629

Share on Facebook (https://www.facebook.com/sharer/sharer.php?u=https%3A//americanmilitarynews.com/2018/03/op-ed-how-the-st-louis-circuit-attorneys-anti-police-agenda-led-to-the-indictment-of-a-pro-cop-republican-governor/&utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=4bfc862de7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-4bfc862de7-99411629)
Share on Twitter (https://twitter.com/home?status=How%20the%20St.%20Louis%20Circuit%20attorney%E2%80%99s%20anti-police%20agenda%20led%20to%20the%20indictment%20of%20a%20pro-cop%20Republican%20governor%20--%0A%20https%3A//americanmilitarynews.com/2018/03/op-ed-how-the-st-louis-circuit-attorneys-anti-police-agenda-led-to-the-indictment-of-a-pro-cop-republican-governor/%20%23moleg&utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=4bfc862de7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-4bfc862de7-99411629)

Paid for by Greitens for Missouri
Jeff Stuerman, Treasurer

This email was sent to dpwillis67+greitens@gmail.com. You can also follow us on Facebook (https://facebook.com/ericgreitens?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=4bfc862de7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-4bfc862de7-99411629) and Twitter (https://twitter.com/ericgreitens?utm_source=Master+List&utm_campaign=4bfc862de7-EMAIL_CAMPAIGN_2018_02_20&utm_medium=email&utm_term=0_3f71c40094-4bfc862de7-99411629) . Emails are one of the best ways to stay connected. If they become too much, you can unsubscribe (https://ericgreitens.us13.list-manage.com/unsubscribe?u=c81357bdb59fb170d9eb82b3a&id=3f71c40094&e=84041254e6&c=4bfc862de7) .

body:
Here's how we stop the Trump agenda, Friends: by taking back the Senate.
_The New York Times_ says the fight for Senate control is officially a "tossup" - but the path to taking back the majority comes down to Nevada.
**With everything that 's at stake under this administration, the fight to elect Democrat Jacky Rosen and flip Nevada's Senate seat could _not_ be more important.**
But CNN says Democrats have "no room for error" - and a recent poll shows the race is a dead heat. That's why groups backed by the Koch brothers and Mitch McConnell are already vowing to spend millions to keep the state red, so Jacky can _only_ win if grassroots supporters like you step up before it's too late.
**We can 't take back the Senate without your support.** **[Please, give $5 right now to help Jacky Rosen flip Nevada blue and take back the Senate for Democrats:](https://secure.actblue.com/contribute/page/cjs4jr?refcode=jtk828-bu-nd&amounts=5,25,50,100,250,500)**

name: The White House
subject: His heart was in America
body:
1600 Daily
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4891d8451710c47614b7c3c9b9800b58f70b9763ab5f9b15a4d1dd34227f1ee9109177c68a45f61c6655181e92e9fa80fafdf91e16a693dc

His heart was in America

“Billy Graham carried his message around the world, but his heart, as [his son] Franklin will tell you, was always in America,” President Donald J. Trump said Wednesday as he stood near the casket of the Reverend Billy Graham, who lay in honor earlier this week at the United States Capitol.

Today, Rev. Graham’s funeral will be held at the Billy Graham Library in Charlotte, North Carolina. On Wednesday, American leaders had an opportunity to pay tribute to the man who inspired millions the world over with his faith.

“He took his message to the poorest places, to the downtrodden and to the brokenhearted, to inmates in prison, and to the overlooked and the neglected,” President Trump told those gathered in the Capitol Rotunda. “He felt a great passion for those that were neglected.”

Rev. Graham is only the fourth private citizen to lie in honor at the U.S. Capitol in Washington.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4891d8451710c4768731aa5e825ff3f57b98332eb302efee77dd7f430e222166fa4d66ec148ef3fb5e3d976a06a2bd445932c851a8948554
Watch the powerful video of Wednesday’s ceremony honoring Rev. Graham.

The pain beneath the headlines

First Lady Melania Trump opened a White House summit on opioids yesterday by putting the Administration’s extensive work to combat the crisis into context.

“Our unified goal is that of helping all who have been affected by drug addiction,” the First Lady said. She then shared a letter from Betty Henderson, a mother who lost her son to drugs. Ms. Henderson joined the First Lady at the front of the room.

“The day after my son’s death, I had to go to the medical examiner’s office to claim his body,” Ms. Henderson wrote. “No mother should ever have to claim their child’s body.”

Yesterday’s summit brought together leaders from across the Administration and experts from throughout the country to discuss education and prevention, treatment and recovery, and law enforcement and interdiction relating to opioids. President Trump spoke with families at the event, and numerous Cabinet officials joined to share policy solutions and ideas from their teams.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4891d8451710c47660cc5e33cf9d102bf9e3b25e0f4091ac0b6afa15c67f59ccb82f140681157cc2364bd8388b5e357f41368baaa5480a3d
Watch Eric Bolling share the story of his son who died from an accidental overdose.

Learn more:
https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4891d8451710c4767d770e357a4d1f74756f845722bc1be1ccdf3638a5747a75d1bc34933e65f7ab814d48421b54c8355e430f6ec3af8297
How President Trump’s Administration is taking on the opioid crisis

Homeland Security turns 15

“I was there on Capitol Hill on 9/11. I watched the Congress come together, setting politics completely aside to forge this Department,” Vice President Mike Pence said yesterday at a ceremony to honor the 15th anniversary of the Department of Homeland Security.

Here are just a few of the ways DHS serves the American people:

- Enforcing our Nation’s immigration laws, securing its borders, and preventing terrorists, drug dealers, and violent criminals from entering our country
- Performing countless search-and-rescue missions across America in the midst of hurricanes, storms, floods, fires, and mudslides
- Protecting the First and Second Family and safeguarding others who serve our Nation through the Secret Service

“This Department was forged in the crucible of crisis. But over the past 15 years it has distinguished itself as a cornerstone of America’s national security and law enforcement enterprise,” the Vice President said.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4891d8451710c476e8ffc97f7649cdcd88621d7efd650a6ac94837ee9481b050483b068989ede4cae48020238859b4a18ab10bc47ca1d2d8
Read more of what Vice President Pence said about DHS’s role in keeping America safe for 15 years.

Photo of the Day

First Lady Melania Trump and Betty Henderson at the White House’s Opioids Summit | March 1, 2018 (Official White House Photo by Andrea Hanks)

POTUS TODAY

Today, President Trump, the First Lady, Vice President and Second Lady will attend the funeral of the Reverend Billy Graham.

This afternoon, the Vice President will deliver remarks at the America First Policies “Tax Cuts to Put America First” event.

https://click.mail.whitehouse.gov/?qs=4891d8451710c4765b2f43ddf5ca498a0a6494cfb2ef28c8f3dfac9d6aaf79fdcbae0728814295fd82db42af29d1889f6bca505dde42a528

name: Sara Imam via Democrats.org
subject: I am a Parkland student survivor
body:
On February 14th, I lost 17 members of my community in a mass shooting at my school, Marjory Stoneman Douglas. But we, the survivors, refuse to let their names be forgotten. We refuse to let their dea
---
On February 14th, I lost 17 members of my community in a mass shooting at my school, Marjory Stoneman Douglas. But we, the survivors, refuse to let their names be forgotten. We refuse to let their deaths be in vain.

We have decided to take action to ensure that an event like this will never happen again. We are calling for stricter gun laws. We are calling for change.

I turned 18 the day after the shooting, and I immediately registered to vote. But many of my fellow survivors can't vote yet, and we are counting on you to stand up for young people at the ballot box.

This week the Democratic Party launched IWillVote -- a national campaign to organize voters and get them to the polls. Voting is the first step toward ending gun violence, so I'd like to ask you to do something important today.

**Will you add your name right now to commit to vote this fall?**

**[COMMIT TO VOTE](https://my.democrats. "https://my.democrats.org/page/s/IWillVote?keycode=&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=1+-+COMMIT+TO+VOTE&utm_campaign=em_20180302_si_dems&source=em_20180302_si_dems")**
Students like me are protesting, marching, and speaking out for our right to live free from the fear of gun violence. We are talking to our elected officials and debating the president himself to demand new gun laws that will keep us safe.

We've kept up the momentum of our #NeverAgain movement -- but the truth is many of us are not old enough to vote.

We need you, the people of America, to get out and vote this year -- for you, your family, your community, but also for people like me. Do it for the survivors who can't vote yet. Do it for those who will never be able to vote because they were murdered in their school, their neighborhood, or their place of worship. Do it so no parent will ever have to receive the news that their child has been shot at school.

There is too much at stake for us to retreat from this fight. Can I count on you to commit to vote this fall?

**[Add your name right now to commit to vote in the fall. It's up to every single one of us to organize our friends, family, and neighbors to go to the polls.](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052274a/4067b0a/57f024088/7e56264d/782966694/VEsE/p/eyJKU1ZtYVhKemRHNWhiV1VsSlE9PSI6IkRlcmVrIiwiSlNWc1lYTjBibUZ0WlNVbCI6IldpbGxpcyIsIkpTVmxiV0ZwYkNVbCI6ImRwd2lsbGlzNjdAZ21haWwuY29tIiwiSlNWNmFYQWxKUT09IjoiMjAxMTIifQ==/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/s/IWillVote?&firstname=Derek&lastname=Willis&email=dpwillis67@gmail.com&zip=20112&utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=2+-+Add+your+name+right+now+to+commit+to+vot&utm_campaign=em_20180302_si_dems&source=em_20180302_si_dems")**

I will never lose my sense of outrage over our country's gun violence problem -- and I will never stop fighting to make sure our elected officials do something about it. I hope you won't either.

In solidarity,

Sara

Sara Imam
Marjory Stoneman Douglas Student and Survivor

Paid for by the Democratic National Committee, 430 South Capitol Street SE, Washington DC 20003 and not authorized by any candidate or candidate's committee.

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If this isn't the best email address at which to reach you, [update your contact information](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052274a/4067b0a/57f024088/7e56264a/782966694/VEsF/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/user/manage?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=3+-+update+your+contact+information&utm_campaign=em_20180302_si_dems&source=em_20180302_si_dems"). Our email list is the best way we have of staying in regular contact with supporters like you across the country and letting you know about the work Democrats are doing. If you like staying in touch, but want to receive only the most important messages, click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052274a/4067b0a/57f024088/7e56264b/782966694/VEsC/ "https://my.democrats.org/page/signup/less-email?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=4+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180302_si_dems&source=em_20180302_si_dems"). Click [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052274a/4067b0a/57f024088/7e562648/782966694/VEsD/ "http://www.democrats.org/unsubscribe?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=5+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180302_si_dems&source=em_20180302_si_dems") to unsubscribe from our supporter list, but if you leave, it will be harder for you to stay involved in the organization that you've been such a critical part of. This organization is powered by you, and we'd love to hear your ideas. Send us any comments, criticisms, or feedback [here](https://my.democrats.org/page/m/4052274a/4067b0a/57f024088/7e562649/782966694/VEsA/ "http://my.democrats.org/page/s/contact-the-democrats?utm_medium=email&utm_source=dnc&utm_content=6+-+here&utm_campaign=em_20180302_si_dems&source=em_20180302_si_dems"), or just reply to this email! Thanks for supporting our party.

Slashing these critical programs so that the top one percent become wealthier and corporations increase their profits is unacceptable

Millions of Americans nationwide rely on Medicare and Medicaid. We shouldn't be cutting those programs, we should protect them!

name: Team Jealous
subject: You're Invited: Meet & Greet with Candidate for Lt. Governor, Susan Turnbull
disclaimer_text: PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
body:
#
Friends,
Susan Turnbull is running for Lt. Governor with Ben Jealous, former National President & CEO of the NAACP. She'll be in Montgomery County **on Saturday, March 3rd** meeting with members of the community to discuss fully funding education, protecting our environment, and pushing back against Trump's policies at the state level.
[](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a730/7ab0e2c4/7f42574b/349a0f7b/3649638183/VEsH/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/meet-greet-susie?source=EMD03028A")
Susie has dedicated over 40 years to community activism, civil rights, political organizing and creating opportunities for everyday people to access their government. She has worked in a variety of governmental, non-profit, business and political positions, including Vice Chair of the Democratic National Committee under Howard Dean and Chairwoman of the Maryland Democratic Party.
Saturday, join the discussion with Susie in Montgomery County. **[RSVP here to let us know you're coming.](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a730/7ab0e2c4/7f42574b/349a0f7b/3649638183/VEsE/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/meet-greet-susie?source=EMD03028A") **
Hope to see you there,
Team Jealous
Saturday, March 3rd, 9:00-10:30 am
923 Gist Ave, Silver Spring MD 20910
**[Please RSVP here](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a730/7ab0e2c4/7f42574b/349a0f7b/3649638183/VEsF/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/meet-greet-susie?source=EMD03028A")**
****[CHIP IN TODAY](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a730/7ab0e2c4/7f42574b/349a0f7b/3649638183/VEsC/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/meet-greet-susie?source=EMD03028A")****

* * *
PAID FOR BY FRIENDS OF BEN JEALOUS, JASON SMALL, TREASURER
---

This email was sent to dpwillis67@gmail.com. If you need to update or change your information or email address, [click here](http://act.benjealous.com/page/m/5c37a730/7ab0e2c4/7f42574b/349a0f7a/3649638183/VEsD/ "http://act.benjealous.com/page/s/update-your-information?source=emfooter"). **NH REPUBLICAN PARTY**
**WEEKLY NEWSLETTER**
**FRIDAY, MARCH 2ND**
|
---
**_A Note From Jeanie_**
**Dear Derek,**

**This past Tuesday, Republicans lost another seat in what I expect will be the last Special Election before the 2018 election cycle.**

**While it was a disappointing result, I am proud of the thoughtful planning, hard work, and collaboration to get a good Republican elected. We knocked on nearly 10,000 doors and made over 10,000 calls with over 60 volunteers (from all around the state and as far away as Colebrook) stepping up to help.**

**Our campaign plan included analysis and focus on unique universes, paid advertising including print and radio, a very successful event with Governor Sununu drawing nearly 100 people to the VFW in Laconia, and a candidate who was highly qualified, with a great message, and willing to work hard.**
**
**
**In the end, with a 17% turnout, we fell 127 votes short. We failed to get 127 Republicans to the polls to vote for our candidate.**

**What's frustrating is I know we are better than this. We had a better candidate, we did the work required, but Republicans did not turn out to vote.**

**As we move into the 2018 election cycle, Republicans CANNOT afford to sit back and be complacent. The Democrats aren't smarter or more qualified, they are just motivated because they are in the minority -- in the House, Senate, and Executive Council, and they don't hold the Corner Office.**

**But in a blink of an eye, we could see that all change. We could wake up on November 7 and see all the good work we've achieved go right down the drain.**

**Under Republican leadership we've lowered business taxes, and reduced burdensome regulations. Under Republican leadership we have school choice and constitutional carry. We've achieved so much -- are we going fight to make sure we continue to govern in 2019? I want to be able to say more than "I hope so." I want to be able to say "of course we are!"**

**Many thanks to all those who helped Les Cartier run in the Special Election in Laconia. Without all those who stayed up late, put signs up in the dark snowy night, made phone calls, knocked on doors, and donated, we could not have come as close as we did.**

**Sincerely, **

**Jeanie**

clean_body: | | nh.gop NH REPUBLICAN PARTY WEEKLY NEWSLETTER FRIDAY, MARCH 2ND | A Note From Jeanie Dear Derek, This past Tuesday, Republicans lost another seat in what I expect will be the last Special Election before the 2018 election cycle. While it was a disappointing result, I am proud of the thoughtful planning, hard work, and collaboration to get a good Republican elected. We knocked on nearly 10,000 doors and made over 10,000 calls with over 60 volunteers (from all around the state and as far away as Colebrook) stepping up to help. Our campaign plan included analysis and focus on unique universes, paid advertising including print and radio, a very successful event with Governor Sununu drawing nearly 100 people to the VFW in Laconia, and a candidate who was highly qualified, with a great message, and willing to work hard. In the end, with a 17% turnout, we fell 127 votes short. We failed to get 127 Republicans to the polls to vote for our candidate. What's frustrating is I know we are better than this. We had a better candidate, we did the work required, but Republicans did not turn out to vote. As we move into the 2018 election cycle, Republicans CANNOT afford to sit back and be complacent. The Democrats aren't smarter or more qualified, they are just motivated because they are in the minority -- in the House, Senate, and Executive Council, and they don't hold the Corner Office. But in a blink of an eye, we could see that all change. We could wake up on November 7 and see all the good work we've achieved go right down the drain. Under Republican leadership we've lowered business taxes, and reduced burdensome regulations. Under Republican leadership we have school choice and constitutional carry. We've achieved so much -- are we going fight to make sure we continue to govern in 2019? I want to be able to say more than "I hope so." I want to be able to say "of course we are!" Many thanks to all those who helped Les Cartier run in the Special Election in Laconia. Without all those who stayed up late, put signs up in the dark snowy night, made phone calls, knocked on doors, and donated, we could not have come as close as we did. Sincerely, Jeanie Join the Grassroots Circle! The Grassroots Circle is a unique way for you to get involved while supporting the Party. Becoming a member couldn't be easier -- join today for as little as $10 per month. There are three levels of support: The Grassroots Supporter, the NHGOP Advocate and the VIP Investor.
